# LiftOn vs Liftoff vs miniprot — Benchmark Comparison

Three-way comparison of standalone **Liftoff**, standalone **miniprot**, and **LiftOn** (reusing both via `-L`/`-M`) across 8 chromosome-subset benchmarks. Primary metrics from a custom evaluator reusing LiftOn's own parasail kernel (`align.py` + `get_id_fraction.py`); cross-checked with `liftofftools variants`. Benchmarks: Human GRCh38 -> CHM13 (MANE), Honey bee HAv3.1 -> ASM1932182v1, Mouse GRCm39 -> NOD_SCID, Human GRCh38 -> Chimp NHGRI_mPanTro3-v1.1, Mouse GRCm39 -> Rat mRatBN7.2, Rice IRGSP -> ASM3414082v1, Arabidopsis TAIR10 -> ASM2311539v1, D. melanogaster -> D. erecta.

> **Reading the metrics.** *Completeness (coding)* = recovered coding transcripts / reference coding transcripts. *Protein identity* is CDS-translated and directly comparable across tools. *DNA identity* basis is full-transcript (exon-spliced) for Liftoff/LiftOn but CDS-spliced for miniprot (it has no UTR/exon records) — see the `DNA basis` column. Cross-species benchmarks subset both genomes to one syntenic chromosome, so non-syntenic reference genes count as lost by design.

## 1. Master comparison

| Benchmark | Tool | Completeness (coding) | Mean prot id | Median prot id | % identical | Mean DNA id | Wall (s) | Peak RSS (MB) | DNA basis |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| human_mane | liftoff | 99.8% | 0.993 | 1.000 | 72.3% | 0.998 | 15.3 | 553 | transcript |
| human_mane | miniprot | 100.0% | 0.990 | 1.000 | 61.0% | 0.995 | 2.3 | 722 | cds |
| human_mane | lifton | 99.8% | 0.994 | 1.000 | 72.3% | 0.998 | 20.0 | 1501 | transcript |
| bee | liftoff | 99.7% | 0.982 | 1.000 | 73.0% | 0.996 | 32.8 | 1267 | transcript |
| bee | miniprot | 99.8% | 0.981 | 1.000 | 63.3% | 0.996 | 4.3 | 1532 | cds |
| bee | lifton | 99.7% | 0.992 | 1.000 | 73.1% | 0.996 | 224.3 | 11006 | transcript |
| mouse | liftoff | 94.0% | 0.990 | 1.000 | 74.4% | 0.994 | 152.3 | 3308 | transcript |
| mouse | miniprot | 95.7% | 0.980 | 1.000 | 60.8% | 0.988 | 33.8 | 2082 | cds |
| mouse | lifton | 94.0% | 0.994 | 1.000 | 74.4% | 0.994 | 1846.2 | 44856 | transcript |
| human_to_chimp | liftoff | 99.0% | 0.965 | 0.995 | 20.6% | 0.984 | 301.4 | 4745 | transcript |
| human_to_chimp | miniprot | 99.9% | 0.965 | 0.994 | 15.8% | 0.989 | 60.5 | 6075 | cds |
| human_to_chimp | lifton | 99.0% | 0.981 | 0.995 | 20.7% | 0.984 | 1250.1 | 11269 | transcript |
| mouse_to_rat | liftoff | 90.4% | 0.859 | 0.958 | 4.9% | 0.875 | 497.6 | 5049 | transcript |
| mouse_to_rat | miniprot | 93.6% | 0.876 | 0.955 | 4.1% | 0.922 | 38.3 | 2200 | cds |
| mouse_to_rat | lifton | 90.4% | 0.910 | 0.958 | 4.9% | 0.875 | 1869.0 | 44692 | transcript |
| rice | liftoff | 100.0% | 0.999 | 1.000 | 99.5% | 1.000 | 31.2 | 926 | transcript |
| rice | miniprot | 100.0% | 0.995 | 1.000 | 91.6% | 0.998 | 4.5 | 606 | cds |
| rice | lifton | 100.0% | 0.999 | 1.000 | 99.5% | 1.000 | 123.0 | 1504 | transcript |
| arabidopsis | liftoff | 99.8% | 0.999 | 1.000 | 99.5% | 1.000 | 81.1 | 2280 | transcript |
| arabidopsis | miniprot | 100.0% | 0.996 | 1.000 | 92.3% | 0.997 | 8.1 | 402 | cds |
| arabidopsis | lifton | 99.8% | 0.999 | 1.000 | 99.5% | 1.000 | 196.9 | 1494 | transcript |
| drosophila | liftoff | 98.2% | 0.908 | 0.958 | 4.8% | 0.890 | 124.2 | 3133 | transcript |
| drosophila | miniprot | 99.0% | 0.921 | 0.958 | 4.1% | 0.919 | 6.7 | 431 | cds |
| drosophila | lifton | 98.2% | 0.925 | 0.958 | 4.8% | 0.890 | 241.8 | 2502 | transcript |


## 1b. All-feature-type completeness

Beyond coding transcripts, this tracks recovery of **every** reference feature type (genes, mRNAs, ncRNAs, tRNAs, rRNAs, lncRNAs, pseudogenes, …) by id. LiftOn inherits Liftoff's full-annotation lift, so the two track closely; miniprot is protein-only (emits its own ids) and is excluded from id-recovery here — its coding recovery is in the master table above.

| Benchmark | Liftoff all-feature compl. | LiftOn all-feature compl. | Δ (LiftOn−Liftoff) | Ref features |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| human_mane | 99.8% | 99.8% | +0.0% | 846 |
| bee | 96.7% | 96.7% | +0.0% | 5398 |
| mouse | 78.8% | 78.8% | +0.0% | 17291 |
| human_to_chimp | 32.3% | 32.3% | +0.0% | 64128 |
| mouse_to_rat | 73.4% | 73.4% | +0.0% | 17291 |
| rice | 97.6% | 97.6% | +0.0% | 11905 |
| arabidopsis | 91.6% | 91.6% | +0.0% | 25276 |
| drosophila | 90.8% | 90.8% | +0.0% | 13157 |


<img alt="all-feature completeness" src="data:image/png;base64," style="max-width:100%;">


**Per-feature-type detail — MAIN benchmark (`human_mane`):**

| Feature type | Ref n | Liftoff recovered | Liftoff % | LiftOn recovered | LiftOn % |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| gene | 423 | 422 | 99.8% | 422 | 99.8% |
| mRNA | 423 | 422 | 99.8% | 422 | 99.8% |


## 1c. Feature-mode comparison (genes vs allfeat)

Two maintained benchmark sets: **genes** runs Liftoff with only the gene hierarchy (default); **allfeat** runs Liftoff with `-f` listing every top-level annotation type (pseudogenes, mobile elements, ncRNA genes, regulatory features, …) and passes the same `-f` to LiftOn. This shows how many more features the all-feature lift recovers — the gap iteration-1 surfaced.

| Benchmark | Tool | Genes all-feat compl. | AllFeat all-feat compl. | Δ | Newly-lifted types | Extra recovered |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| human_mane | liftoff | 99.8% | 99.8% | +0.0% | — | 0 |
| human_mane | lifton | 99.8% | 99.8% | +0.0% | — | 0 |
| bee | liftoff | 96.7% | 96.7% | +0.0% | pseudogene | +1 |
| bee | lifton | 96.7% | 96.7% | +0.0% | pseudogene | +1 |
| mouse | liftoff | 78.8% | 87.9% | +9.1% | DNaseI_hypersensitive_site, biological_region, conserved_region, enhancer, enhancer_blocking_element, matrix_attachment_site, origin_of_replication, promoter, protein_binding_site, pseudogene, transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region | +1576 |
| mouse | lifton | 78.8% | 87.9% | +9.1% | DNaseI_hypersensitive_site, biological_region, conserved_region, enhancer, enhancer_blocking_element, matrix_attachment_site, origin_of_replication, promoter, protein_binding_site, pseudogene, transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region | +1576 |
| human_to_chimp | liftoff | 32.3% | 71.9% | +39.6% | CAGE_cluster, DNaseI_hypersensitive_site, biological_region, conserved_region, enhancer, enhancer_blocking_element, epigenetically_modified_region, locus_control_region, meiotic_recombination_region, minisatellite, mobile_genetic_element, non_allelic_homologous_recombination_region, promoter, protein_binding_site, pseudogene, repeat_instability_region, response_element, silencer, tandem_repeat, transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region | +25384 |
| human_to_chimp | lifton | 32.3% | 71.9% | +39.6% | CAGE_cluster, DNaseI_hypersensitive_site, biological_region, conserved_region, enhancer, enhancer_blocking_element, epigenetically_modified_region, locus_control_region, meiotic_recombination_region, minisatellite, mobile_genetic_element, non_allelic_homologous_recombination_region, promoter, protein_binding_site, pseudogene, repeat_instability_region, response_element, silencer, tandem_repeat, transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region | +25384 |
| mouse_to_rat | liftoff | 73.4% | 78.4% | +5.0% | DNaseI_hypersensitive_site, biological_region, conserved_region, enhancer, enhancer_blocking_element, origin_of_replication, promoter, protein_binding_site, pseudogene, transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region | +870 |
| mouse_to_rat | lifton | 73.4% | 78.4% | +5.0% | DNaseI_hypersensitive_site, biological_region, conserved_region, enhancer, enhancer_blocking_element, origin_of_replication, promoter, protein_binding_site, pseudogene, transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region | +870 |
| rice | liftoff | 97.6% | 99.1% | +1.6% | pseudogene | +185 |
| rice | lifton | 97.6% | 99.1% | +1.6% | pseudogene | +185 |
| arabidopsis | liftoff | 91.6% | 99.0% | +7.4% | pseudogene, sequence_feature | +1862 |
| arabidopsis | lifton | 91.6% | 99.0% | +7.4% | pseudogene, sequence_feature | +1862 |
| drosophila | liftoff | 90.8% | 91.4% | +0.5% | mobile_genetic_element, pseudogene | +71 |
| drosophila | lifton | 90.8% | 91.4% | +0.5% | mobile_genetic_element, pseudogene | +71 |


<img alt="genes vs allfeat" src="data:image/png;base64," style="max-width:100%;">


**Feature types LiftOn lifts only in the allfeat set:**

| Benchmark | Newly-lifted type | Ref n | LiftOn recovered (allfeat) | % |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| bee | pseudogene | 3 | 1 | 33.3% |
| mouse | DNaseI_hypersensitive_site | 28 | 28 | 100.0% |
| mouse | biological_region | 416 | 412 | 99.0% |
| mouse | conserved_region | 12 | 11 | 91.7% |
| mouse | enhancer | 433 | 428 | 98.8% |
| mouse | enhancer_blocking_element | 1 | 1 | 100.0% |
| mouse | matrix_attachment_site | 1 | 1 | 100.0% |
| mouse | origin_of_replication | 3 | 3 | 100.0% |
| mouse | promoter | 11 | 11 | 100.0% |
| mouse | protein_binding_site | 77 | 10 | 13.0% |
| mouse | pseudogene | 662 | 625 | 94.4% |
| mouse | transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region | 16 | 9 | 56.2% |
| human_to_chimp | CAGE_cluster | 6 | 6 | 100.0% |
| human_to_chimp | DNaseI_hypersensitive_site | 7 | 6 | 85.7% |
| human_to_chimp | biological_region | 12196 | 11456 | 93.9% |
| human_to_chimp | conserved_region | 3 | 3 | 100.0% |
| human_to_chimp | enhancer | 10009 | 9267 | 92.6% |
| human_to_chimp | enhancer_blocking_element | 1 | 1 | 100.0% |
| human_to_chimp | epigenetically_modified_region | 3 | 3 | 100.0% |
| human_to_chimp | locus_control_region | 2 | 1 | 50.0% |
| human_to_chimp | meiotic_recombination_region | 27 | 26 | 96.3% |
| human_to_chimp | minisatellite | 2 | 1 | 50.0% |
| human_to_chimp | mobile_genetic_element | 5 | 5 | 100.0% |
| human_to_chimp | non_allelic_homologous_recombination_region | 20 | 15 | 75.0% |
| human_to_chimp | promoter | 18 | 12 | 66.7% |
| human_to_chimp | protein_binding_site | 65 | 2 | 3.1% |
| human_to_chimp | pseudogene | 1467 | 1339 | 91.3% |
| human_to_chimp | repeat_instability_region | 3 | 3 | 100.0% |
| human_to_chimp | response_element | 7 | 1 | 14.3% |
| human_to_chimp | silencer | 3540 | 3188 | 90.1% |
| human_to_chimp | tandem_repeat | 1 | 1 | 100.0% |
| human_to_chimp | transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region | 201 | 78 | 38.8% |
| mouse_to_rat | DNaseI_hypersensitive_site | 28 | 28 | 100.0% |
| mouse_to_rat | biological_region | 416 | 227 | 54.6% |
| mouse_to_rat | conserved_region | 12 | 10 | 83.3% |
| mouse_to_rat | enhancer | 433 | 241 | 55.7% |
| mouse_to_rat | enhancer_blocking_element | 1 | 1 | 100.0% |
| mouse_to_rat | origin_of_replication | 3 | 1 | 33.3% |
| mouse_to_rat | promoter | 11 | 11 | 100.0% |
| mouse_to_rat | protein_binding_site | 77 | 2 | 2.6% |
| mouse_to_rat | pseudogene | 662 | 314 | 47.4% |
| mouse_to_rat | transcriptional_cis_regulatory_region | 16 | 6 | 37.5% |
| rice | pseudogene | 158 | 158 | 100.0% |
| arabidopsis | pseudogene | 930 | 903 | 97.1% |
| arabidopsis | sequence_feature | 31 | 24 | 77.4% |
| drosophila | mobile_genetic_element | 691 | 57 | 8.2% |
| drosophila | pseudogene | 44 | 21 | 47.7% |


## 2. Head-to-head — does LiftOn improve on Liftoff?

Positive Δ = LiftOn better. The thesis: LiftOn ≥ Liftoff on protein identity and completeness by fusing miniprot's protein evidence.

| Benchmark | LiftOn compl. | Δ vs Liftoff | LiftOn mean prot id | Δ vs Liftoff | Δ vs miniprot |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| human_mane | 99.8% | +0.0% | 0.994 | +0.001 | +0.005 |
| bee | 99.7% | +0.0% | 0.992 | +0.009 | +0.011 |
| mouse | 94.0% | +0.0% | 0.994 | +0.004 | +0.014 |
| human_to_chimp | 99.0% | +0.0% | 0.981 | +0.016 | +0.015 |
| mouse_to_rat | 90.4% | +0.0% | 0.910 | +0.052 | +0.034 |
| rice | 100.0% | +0.0% | 0.999 | +0.000 | +0.004 |
| arabidopsis | 99.8% | +0.0% | 0.999 | +0.000 | +0.003 |
| drosophila | 98.2% | +0.0% | 0.925 | +0.016 | +0.004 |

## 4. LiftoffTools cross-check

Independent confirmation on the Liftoff/LiftOn outputs (miniprot excluded — no gene hierarchy). `identical` count should track the custom evaluator's %identical × recovered.

| Benchmark | Tool | LiftoffTools identical | LiftoffTools n | Custom %identical×recovered | available |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| human_mane | liftoff | 223 | 423 | 305 | yes |
| human_mane | lifton | 222 | 423 | 305 | yes |
| bee | liftoff | 1514 | 3714 | 2279 | yes |
| bee | lifton | 1515 | 3714 | 2281 | yes |
| mouse | liftoff | 4640 | 11754 | 5715 | yes |
| mouse | lifton | 4640 | 11751 | 5715 | yes |
| human_to_chimp | liftoff | 452 | 18099 | 2626 | yes |
| human_to_chimp | lifton | 452 | 17942 | 2631 | yes |
| mouse_to_rat | liftoff | 1 | 11751 | 360 | yes |
| mouse_to_rat | lifton | 1 | 11751 | 360 | yes |
| rice | liftoff | — | — | 5823 | no |
| rice | lifton | 5825 | 7302 | 5823 | yes |
| arabidopsis | liftoff | — | — | 12573 | no |
| arabidopsis | lifton | 12568 | 15543 | 12573 | yes |
| drosophila | liftoff | 6 | 8281 | 339 | yes |
| drosophila | lifton | 6 | 8279 | 339 | yes |


## 5. Pipeline

Reproduce with `python -m benchmarks.compare.run_compare --all -t 8` (resumable). Per benchmark: subset both genomes to one chromosome → run Liftoff and miniprot standalone → run LiftOn reusing both (`-L`/`-M`) → evaluate each output → cross-check with LiftoffTools → aggregate. Code under `benchmarks/compare/`.
